af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server (quality 80). Slack's team communication platform. Bot tokens (xoxb-) for automated use; user tokens (xoxp-) for user context. Incoming webhooks for simple one-way messaging. OAuth 2.0 with granular scopes for app installations. Rate limits vary per method tier (Tier 1-4) — agents must implement per-method rate tracking. Missing OAuth scopes give cryptic 'missing_scope' errors. Block Kit message format is powerful but verbose. Free workspace 90-day history limit affects search. Retry guidance documented. Excellent documentation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth 2.0 with highly granular scopes (channels:read, chat:write, files:read, users:read, etc.) — excellent least-privilege design. SOC2 Type II, SOC3, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP certified. Bot tokens distinct from user tokens — bots have defined scope boundary. Token via environment variables. Webhook verification with signing secrets. Enterprise-grade security infrastructure for team communication.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Slack status page and enterprise SLA. SOC2/ISO27001/FedRAMP. Stable API with versioned methods. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Per-method rate limits well documented with Tier categories. Rate limit headers in responses. Very mature platform — core business communication infrastructure for millions of teams.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 78). Community Slack MCP server supporting read-first access with optional write. Supports user tokens (xoxp-), bot tokens (xoxb-), and browser session tokens (xoxc-/xoxd-). auth_complexity_score=72 — multiple token types, browser tokens are fragile. IMPORTANT: Stealth mode using user tokens may violate Slack ToS in some workspace configurations — legal review advised. Message posting disabled by default for safety. Local user/channel cache can become stale. Slack API rate limits can cause tool timeouts on large workspaces. GovSlack requires separate FedRAMP configuration.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS via Slack API. CRITICAL: Browser session token (xoxc-/xoxd-) support extracts tokens from browser sessions — fragile, Slack may invalidate without warning, and represents unauthorized API access pattern that may violate ToS. User tokens with stealth mode are another ToS concern. Community Go project — dependency audit required. SSE/HTTP transport requires API key. Prefer bot token auth with proper Slack app registration. No formal security audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Slack API availability and token validity. Browser session tokens can be invalidated by Slack without notice — unreliable for persistent agents. Rate limit timeouts on large workspaces. Local cache can become stale requiring explicit refresh. Community project with no SLA. Cursor-based pagination. Bot token mode is more reliable than browser/user token modes.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 65). Slack workspace export and archive tool using EZ-Login or browser token extraction — no official Slack app required. auth_complexity_score=45 — browser token auth is fragile and not a proper API mechanism. Read-only and archival focused — no posting capability. AGPLv3 license restricts commercial use of derived works. Enterprise workspaces may trigger security alerts. Error handling is basic. No official API backing — relies on undocumented token extraction.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS via Slack's web interface. CRITICAL: Browser session token extraction is not a supported authentication mechanism — tokens are fragile, can be revoked without notice, and may violate Slack ToS and enterprise security policies. Enterprise workspace DLP controls may trigger security alerts. No granular access control. Extracted tokens could expose all user-accessible workspace data. Community Go project — dependency audit required. AGPLv3 licensing concern for commercial use.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Browser token extraction can break if Slack changes session handling (no notice). Enterprise workspace security policies may block token extraction. No retry guidance documented. AGPLv3 license. Appropriate for personal archival from personal/small-team Slack workspaces, not enterprise production use.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Snowflake SQL API and Python connector for cloud data warehouse queries. auth_complexity_score=72 — key-pair auth (RSA private key generation, DER-encoded public key registration in Snowflake) is recommended but complex setup; username/password exists but discouraged. SQL API is async by default — agents must poll statement handle for completion. Warehouse cold start adds 5-30 seconds on first query after auto-suspend. Account identifier format varies (org_name-account_name) — must use correct format. Large result sets are partitioned — agents must fetch all partitions. SDKs in Python, Node, Java, Go, .NET, PHP. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Key-pair auth generates short-lived JWTs — excellent security posture for agents. External OAuth (Okta, Azure AD) for enterprise SSO. SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP certified — broadest compliance suite. RSA private key via secure env var/vault. No per-query scope control — access controlled at database/schema/table level via RBAC. Column-level security available for sensitive data masking.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Snowflake status page and enterprise SLA with FedRAMP. Stable SQL API. Retry guidance documented. Async query polling documented. Warehouse cold start is a known reliability factor — agents must handle. Result set partitioning requires complete fetch logic. Credit consumption monitoring requires separate usage views. Very reliable as enterprise data warehouse infrastructure for large organizations.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Snyk developer security platform — dependency scanning, SAST, container, and IaC security. IMPORTANT: Two API versions (v1 legacy and new REST v3) with different auth and response shapes — use new REST API for new integrations. Organization ID required for most calls — fetch from /orgs endpoint first. Test endpoints (triggering new scans) count against monthly test quotas. Simple API key authentication. OpenAPI spec available. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Vulnerability database updates continuously — sequential scan results may differ.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Personal API tokens and service account tokens org-scoped. OAuth 2.0 for partner integrations with scopes. SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, GDPR compliant. Security-focused company with strong internal security practices. Token via Authorization Bearer/env vars. No fine-grained per-endpoint scoping within org. Enterprise plan available with enhanced security controls.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Snyk status page, SOC2. v1 legacy and new REST v3 coexist — v1 still functional but v3 is canonical for new work. Test quota limits can cause failures for heavy scan automation. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination on REST v3. Vulnerability DB is continuously updated. Generally reliable as core DevSecOps infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Socket Security supply chain risk analysis for npm, PyPI, Maven, Conda. API key as Bearer token — simple authentication. IMPORTANT: Risk scoring is behavioral/heuristic — agents must treat scores as probabilistic, not definitive verdicts. Alert categories (vulnerability, supply-chain, quality, maintenance) have very different severity implications requiring agent context. Package scores can change over time as analysis improves. No fine-grained scope control. Free tier API access is limited. Webhook authenticity verification — confirm signing approach in docs. SDKs in JavaScript and Python.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. API key as Bearer token — org-scoped, no fine-grained scopes. SOC2 certified. Any key holder has full org API access — protect carefully. Webhook delivery signing (verify in docs before trusting webhooks). Security-focused company with appropriate internal security practices. Token via Authorization header/env vars.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable REST API with OpenAPI spec. Package risk scores change over time — cache invalidation needed for security gates. Retry guidance not documented. Cursor-based pagination. Free tier limits require planning. Generally reliable for pre-install package vetting and CI/CD security gates.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. IMPORTANT: Solon is a Java application development framework (Spring alternative), NOT an API or MCP server that agents can call directly. Agents would use Solon by embedding it in a Java application. Documentation primarily in Chinese — potential barrier for non-Chinese developers. Smaller ecosystem than Spring means fewer community resources. Performance claims (700% higher concurrency, 50% memory savings) are self-reported. auth_complexity_score=50 — auth is application-defined, not framework-provided. No agent-callable interface by design.

security_score_components.security_notes: Security is entirely application-defined — Solon provides auth integration modules similar to Spring Security. TLS configured at deployment level. No built-in auth or credential management — applications must implement. Open source Java framework — developer must audit dependencies. Solon plugin ecosystem is smaller than Spring's, reducing available security integrations. No formal security audit of the framework itself.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Framework reliability depends entirely on application implementation. Consistent versioning in open source project. Performance benchmarks self-reported. Smaller community means fewer vetted patterns for edge cases. GraalVM native image compilation improves startup reliability. No SLA as open source framework. Documentation gap (primarily Chinese) may cause reliability issues for non-Chinese teams.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 62 — community project). SonarQube/SonarCloud REST API for code quality and security analysis. User token as Basic Auth username (empty password) — simple but unusual pattern. IMPORTANT: SonarQube and SonarCloud have similar but not identical APIs — verify target platform. Web API explorer only accessible in UI, not documented as external OpenAPI spec. Component keys are project-specific strings, not UUIDs. Quality gate status computed async after analysis — poll for completion. Metric keys are internal identifiers requiring lookup. Rate limits not documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (SonarCloud); self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility. User token via Basic Auth (token as username, empty password) — unconventional but functional. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. No per-resource scope control beyond user-level permissions. SonarQube token via environment variable. Self-hosted SonarQube requires operator security hardening. Community-maintained MCP server should have dependency audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SonarCloud status page, SOC2. SonarQube self-hosted depends on operator infrastructure. API behavior differs between SonarQube and SonarCloud — careful API version management needed. Quality gate status is async. Analysis completion must be polled. Retry guidance not documented. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable for code quality integration once platform differences are accounted for.

af_score_components.notes: Official SonarSource MCP server (quality 80). Connects AI agents to SonarQube Cloud or self-hosted Server for code quality and security findings. SONARQUBE_TOKEN env var required; SonarCloud also needs SONARQUBE_ORG; self-hosted needs SONARQUBE_URL. Selective toolsets recommended to avoid flooding agent context. Token must be passed via env var, not CLI args, to avoid process listing exposure. Pre-existing SonarQube Cloud or Server required. Generally idempotent queries. Rate limits not documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS via SonarQube infrastructure. Official SonarSource maintenance — strong security posture. User token via SONARQUBE_TOKEN env var (explicitly documented against CLI arg usage to prevent exposure). SOC2 compliance inherited from SonarQube platform. Token scope is user-level — org-scoped access. No fine-grained per-resource token scoping. Java MCP server from official vendor with enterprise development practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Official SonarSource maintenance provides strong reliability guarantee. Requires pre-existing SonarQube infrastructure. Analysis queries are idempotent and stable. Breaking changes coordinated with SonarQube API releases. Rate limits not documented. Quality gate status requires polling. Retry guidance not documented. Docker available for consistent deployment.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Sourcegraph cross-repository code search and intelligence platform — GraphQL API only, no REST. Personal access tokens or service accounts. IMPORTANT: GraphQL-only — agents must construct and parse GraphQL queries, not simple REST calls. Sourcegraph uses custom search syntax (regexp, structural, lang: filters) — not standard text search. Batch Changes (large-scale automated edits) can affect hundreds of repositories — test carefully. Code intelligence (go-to-definition, references) requires LSIF/SCIP index upload per repo. Self-hosted required for private code. Rate limits not prominently documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Personal access tokens for users, service accounts for automation, site admin tokens for admin operations. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. No fine-grained scope control on tokens — full access to accessible repositories. Token via Authorization Bearer/env vars. Site admin tokens have full instance control — restrict carefully. Self-hosted instance security is operator responsibility.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Sourcegraph Cloud status page, SOC2. Stable GraphQL API. Batch Changes are irreversible at scale — dry-run strongly advised. LSIF/SCIP index upload required for code intelligence — stale index affects reliability. Cursor-based pagination on most queries. Retry guidance not documented. Self-hosted instance reliability depends on operator infrastructure.
